---
title: "Preparation of UPR Right-to-Health recommendations from UHRI"
subtitle: "Download and pre-processing of the UHRI export into an analysis-ready table"
author: "Anshu Uppal"
date: "2026-06-19"
date-modified: last-modified
format:
        html:
                code-fold: true
                toc: true
                toc-location: left
                toc-depth: 4
                toc-title: "On this page"
                smooth-scroll: true
                embed-resources: true
                grid:
                  body-width: 1100px
                  margin-width: 100px
---

## Purpose

This document downloads the full UHRI dataset and pre-processes it into two tidy,
analysis-ready tables:

- `data/UHRI_full.rds` — every recommendation/observation (all bodies);
- `data/UHRI_UPR.rds` — the Universal Periodic Review (UPR) subset, with the cleaned
  recommendation text (`text_2`) and parsed identifiers used downstream.

The **thematic classification** of these recommendations is a separate step and lives in
`03_classify_UHRI_recommendations.qmd`, which consumes `data/UHRI_UPR.rds`.

> This is a refactor of `02_prep_UHRI_recommendation_definitions.qmd`. It is **behaviour-equivalent**
> to the original pre-processing (verified by running both pipelines on the same raw extract and
> diffing the outputs) but the code is reorganised into small, documented helper functions and the
> ~25 sequential text substitutions are collapsed into single vectorised passes.

## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
pacman::p_load(
  here,
  tidyverse,
  janitor,
  readxl,
  httr,
  sf
)
```

## 1. Acquire the raw extract

The full UHRI export is documented at <https://uhri.ohchr.org/en/our-data-api>; the direct
download is <https://dataex.ohchr.org/uhri/export-results/export-full-en.xlsx>.

To refresh the data, uncomment the download block below (it overwrites the cached
`data/UHRI_extract_raw.rds`). Otherwise the most recently downloaded extract is reused, which
keeps the pipeline fully reproducible between refreshes.

```{r}
#| label: download

# --- Uncomment to download a fresh extract -----------------------------------------------------
# httr::GET(
#   "https://dataex.ohchr.org/uhri/export-results/export-full-en.xlsx",
#   httr::write_disk(tf <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx"))
# )
# df_0 <- readxl::read_xlsx(
#   tf,
#   # specify column types explicitly, otherwise some columns import as all-NA
#   col_types = c(rep("text", 7), "date", rep("text", 7), "date")
# ) |>
#   janitor::clean_names() |>
#   mutate(date_accessed = ymd(Sys.Date()))
# 
# unlink(tf); rm(tf)
# saveRDS(df_0, here("data", "UHRI_extract_raw.rds"))
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

df_0 <- readRDS(here("data", "UHRI_extract_raw.rds"))

# combine the raw extract with manually extracted recommendations
# (see the folder data/UPR_WG_docs/)
df_0_merged <- bind_rows(df_0, readRDS(here("data", "UPR_WG_docs", "recs_combined.rds"))) |> 
  mutate(
    across(c(provisional, manual_upload), \(x) case_when(is.na(x) ~ FALSE, .default = x))
     )

df_0_merged_test <- df_0_merged |>
  mutate(countries_concerned = str_remove(countries_concerned, "- "),
    upr_session        = str_split_i(str_remove(upr_session, "- "), " - ", 1),
    upr_session_number = as.numeric(str_extract(upr_session, "\\d+")))

# check for duplicates in manually extracted recommendations present in raw extract.
overlap_check <- df_0_merged_test |>
  group_by(countries_concerned, upr_session_number) |>
  summarise(
    has_official = any(manual_upload == FALSE),
    has_manual   = any(manual_upload == TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(has_official & has_manual)

if (nrow(overlap_check) > 0) {
  msg <- overlap_check |>
    mutate(line = paste0("- ", countries_concerned, " (session number ", upr_session_number, ")")) |>
    pull(line) |>
    paste(collapse = "\n")
  
  stop(
    "Duplicate coverage detected: the following country/session pairs have both an official ",
    "and a manual entry. Please check the corresponding files before proceeding:\n",
    msg
  )
}

# Country/region reference layer (ISO codes) used to enrich the recommendations.
state_geo <- readRDS(here("output", "state_geo_enhanced.rds"))
```

## 2. Helper functions

All of the fiddly string work is isolated here so the main pipeline reads as a sequence of
named steps.

### 2.1 Text cleaning

The raw `text` field contains HTML entities, smart quotes, and a handful of recurring OCR-style
glitches. These are fixed with two ordered substitution tables (applied in a single vectorised
`str_replace_all` each) plus a few structural tidies.

```{r}
#| label: helper-clean-text
# Ordered substitutions: HTML entities, smart quotes, spacing around parentheses/possessives.
# str_replace_all() with a named vector applies these in order, element-wise - equivalent to the
# original's long sequence of individual str_replace_all() calls, in one pass.
html_entity_subs <- c(
  "’"           = "'",          # right single quote
  "“"           = "'",          # left double quote
  "”"           = "'",          # right double quote
  "\\( "             = "\\(",        # "( " -> "("
  " \\)"             = "\\)",        # " )" -> ")"
  " ' s "            = "'s ",
  "' s "             = "'s ",
  "&nbsp;"           = " ",
  "&nbsp"            = " ",
  "&rsquo;"          = "'",
  "&ndash;|&#8209;"  = "-",
  "&ocirc;"          = "ô",
  "&ldquo;|&rdquo;"  = "'",
  "&uacute;"         = "ú",
  "&uuml;"           = "ü",
  "&amp;"            = "&",
  "&deg;"            = "°",
  "&eacute;"         = "é",
  "&egrave;"         = "è",
  "&lrm;"            = "",
  "&iacute;"         = "í"
)

# Recurring word-splitting glitches, fixed after squishing whitespace.
typo_subs <- c(
  "non- communicable"          = "non-communicable",
  "l aw s on trans i tional"   = "laws on transitional",
  "sexual a buse"              = "sexual abuse",
  "ex ploit"                   = "exploit",
  "heal thcare"                = "healthcare",
  " h ealth"                   = " health"
)

clean_text <- function(x) {
  x |>
    str_replace_all(html_entity_subs) |>
    str_squish() |>
    str_replace("^R ", "") |>          # drop a stray leading "R " (only at the start)
    str_remove_all("\\[...\\]") |>     # drop "[...]" elision markers (3 chars inside brackets)
    str_replace_all(typo_subs)
}
```

### 2.2 Parse the paragraph / recommendation identifier

Recommendations carry an identifier prefix in several formats. A single engine,
`parse_recommendation()`, returns **both** the normalised identifier (e.g. `96.21`) and the
prefix-stripped recommendation text from the *same* per-format regular expression, so the two can
never disagree. `derive_paragraph()` and `strip_prefix()` are thin wrappers that select the
relevant column.

The recognised formats, tried in order (first match wins), are:

| Format | Example | Identifier |
|---|---|---|
| `Recommendation No.` | `Recommendation No. 41, para. 81: 41. Advance…` | `81.41` (`para.rec`) |
| `contained in / from / of para` | `Recommendations contained in para. 79(a): 79(a). Take…` | `79(a)` |
| `contained in <n>` (no "para") | `Recommendation contained in 25(a): Take…` | `25(a)` |
| `para. <p>` | `paragraph 28: …` | `28` |
| `<a>.<b>` (the dominant UPR form) | `137.197 Improve…` | `137.197` |
| single number | `67. To continue…` | `67` |

Design choices that fixed real data issues:

- The `Recommendation No.` identifier reads the `No.` and `para.` numbers **directly** (the old
  regex assumed the recommendation number was repeated after the colon, producing `paragraph =
  "NA.NA"` and an un-stripped text whenever it was not — 166 rows).
- A trailing **repeated** report number (`… 81: 41. <text>`, including lettered ones like `79(a).`)
  is consumed as part of the prefix, replacing the old blind "strip any leading number" passes that
  could eat genuine content (e.g. "`5 years…`").
- The single-number rule requires 1–3 digits, so years (`2030 Agenda…`) are not mistaken for an id.

```{r}
#| label: helper-paragraph
# One engine -> identifier + stripped text from the same regex per format (no drift between them).
parse_recommendation <- function(text) {
  n         <- length(text)
  paragraph <- rep(NA_character_, n)
  text_2    <- text
  resolved  <- rep(FALSE, n)

  # Apply one format to the not-yet-resolved rows: `re` is the prefix (with capture groups), and
  # `id_of(m)` maps its str_match() matrix to the normalised identifier.
  apply_fmt <- function(re, id_of) {
    todo <- which(!resolved)
    if (!length(todo)) return(invisible())
    m   <- str_match(text[todo], re)
    hit <- !is.na(m[, 1])
    if (!any(hit)) return(invisible())
    idx <- todo[hit]
    paragraph[idx] <<- id_of(m[hit, , drop = FALSE])
    text_2[idx]    <<- str_remove(text[idx], re)   # strip exactly the matched prefix
    resolved[idx]  <<- TRUE
  }

  # F1: "Recommendation No. <rec>[(x)][, contained in] para <p>[: <rep>[(x)].]"  -> "<p>.<rec>"
  #     (prefer the repeated report number for <rec> when present).
  apply_fmt(
    "^Recommendations?\\s*No\\s*\\.?\\s*(\\d+)(\\([a-z]\\))?\\s*,?\\s*(?:(?:contained|conatined) in\\s*)?para(?:graph|s)?\\.?\\s*(?:No\\.?\\s*)?(\\d+)\\s*:?\\s*(?:(\\d+)(\\([a-z]\\))?\\s*\\.\\s*)?",
    \(m) {
      use_rep <- !is.na(m[, 5]) & m[, 5] != ""
      str_c(m[, 4], ".",
            ifelse(use_rep, m[, 5], m[, 2]),
            ifelse(use_rep, coalesce(m[, 6], ""), coalesce(m[, 3], "")))
    }
  )
  # F2a: "Recommendation(s) contained in / from / of para <p>[(x)] ... : [<rep>[(x)].]"
  apply_fmt(
    "^Recommendations?\\s+(?:(?:contained|conatined) in|from|of)\\s+para(?:graph|s)?\\.?\\s*(?:No\\.?\\s*)?(\\d+)(\\([a-z]\\))?[^:]*:\\s*(?:\\d+(?:\\([a-z]\\))?\\s*\\.\\s*)?",
    \(m) str_c(m[, 2], coalesce(m[, 3], ""))
  )
  # F2b: same, but with no colon (also consumes extra "para. <n>" refs and stray "br>").
  apply_fmt(
    "^Recommendations?\\s+(?:(?:contained|conatined) in|from|of)\\s+para(?:graph|s)?\\.?\\s*(?:No\\.?\\s*)?(\\d+)(\\([a-z]\\))?(?:\\s*,?\\s*(?:para(?:graph|s)?\\.?\\s*)?\\d+(?:\\([a-z]\\))?)*\\s*(?:br>\\s*)?",
    \(m) str_c(m[, 2], coalesce(m[, 3], ""))
  )
  # F2c: "Recommendation(s) contained in <p>[(x)] ... :"  (no "para")
  apply_fmt(
    "^Recommendations?\\s+(?:contained|conatined) in\\s+(\\d+)(\\([a-z]\\))?[^:]*:\\s*(?:\\d+(?:\\([a-z]\\))?\\s*\\.\\s*)?",
    \(m) str_c(m[, 2], coalesce(m[, 3], ""))
  )
  # F3: "para. <p>[(x)][: <rep>.]"
  apply_fmt(
    "^[Pp]ara(?:graph|s)?\\s*\\.?\\s*(\\d+)(\\([a-z]\\))?\\s*[:.]?\\s*(?:\\d+(?:\\([a-z]\\))?\\s*\\.\\s*)?",
    \(m) str_c(m[, 2], coalesce(m[, 3], ""))
  )
  # F4: "<a>.<b> ..."  (dominant UPR form)
  apply_fmt("^(\\d+\\.\\d+)\\.?\\s*", \(m) m[, 2])
  # F5: single number (1-3 digits, so years are not captured), period or space separator
  apply_fmt("^(\\d{1,3})[.\\s]+", \(m) m[, 2])

  # Final tidy: drop any leading stray punctuation, then a single leftover "<n>. " numbering
  # artifact (period-terminated, so "5 years ..." is left intact).
  text_2 <- text_2 |>
    str_remove("^[\\s.:;]+") |>
    str_remove("^\\d{1,3}\\s*\\.\\s+") |>
    str_squish()

  tibble(paragraph = paragraph, text_2 = text_2)
}

# Thin wrappers keep the call sites in the pipeline unchanged.
derive_paragraph <- function(text) parse_recommendation(text)$paragraph
strip_prefix     <- function(text) parse_recommendation(text)$text_2
```

### 2.3 Sortable numeric key and recommending-state list

```{r}
#| label: helper-misc
# UPR recommending states are stored as a single "-"-delimited string. Re-join them with a chosen
# separator (vectorised; replaces the original separate_wider_delim()/unite() round-trip).
collapse_states <- function(x, sep) {
  na_if(map_chr(str_split(x, "-"), \(parts) str_c(str_squish(parts), collapse = sep)), "")
}
```

The sortable key is built from a `title_a` (paragraph number) and `title_b` (zero-padded
sub-number) which are retained as columns for compatibility with downstream use.

## 3. Build the full table

```{r}
#| label: build-full
df <- df_0_merged |>
  mutate(
    countries_concerned = str_remove(countries_concerned, "- "),
    reccomending_body   = str_remove(reccomending_body, "- ")
  ) |>
  # Clean the recommendation text; keep the pre-clean version in `text1`.
  mutate(text1 = text, text = clean_text(text)) |>
  # Correct one mis-imported duplicate entry.
  mutate(text = if_else(
    text == "137.197 Improve the socioeconomic situation of the Roma national minority, also by ensuring adequate living conditions and access to education and health care (Germany);",
    "137.197 Fully implement the right to equal usage of minority languages and scripts, in compliance with Croatia’s legal framework, with a special focus on those regions where this right especially applies (Germany);",
    text
  )) |>
  arrange(countries_concerned, reccomending_body, document_publication_date) |>
  rename(document = document_symbol, body = reccomending_body) |>
  # Parse identifiers and UPR session/cycle metadata.
  mutate(
    paragraph          = derive_paragraph(text),
    upr_session        = str_split_i(str_remove(upr_session, "- "), " - ", 1),
    upr_session_number = as.numeric(str_extract(upr_session, "\\d+")),
    upr_cycle = case_when(
      upr_session_number <= 12 ~ "Cycle 1",
      upr_session_number <= 26 ~ "Cycle 2",
      upr_session_number <= 40 ~ "Cycle 3",
      upr_session_number <= 54 ~ "Cycle 4"
    ),
    type         = str_remove(type, "- "),
    upr_position = str_remove(upr_position, "- "),
    # Sortable numeric key "<a>.<bbb>" from the paragraph id (title_a/title_b kept for downstream).
    title_a = str_split_i(paragraph, "\\.", 1),
    title_a = case_when(is.na(as.numeric(title_a)) ~ str_extract(title_a, "^\\d+"),
                        .default = title_a),
    title_b = str_pad(str_split_i(paragraph, "\\.", 2), width = 3, side = "left", pad = "0"),
    title_b = case_when(is.na(title_b) | str_starts(title_b, "\\(") ~ "0",
                        .default = title_b),
    title_2 = as.numeric(paste(title_a, title_b, sep = "."))
  ) |>
  # Order the session/cycle factors by session number.
  arrange(upr_session_number) |>
  mutate(
    upr_session = fct_inorder(upr_session),
    upr_cycle   = fct_inorder(upr_cycle)
  ) |>
  relocate(paragraph, .after = document) |>
  relocate(title_2, upr_position, type, .after = paragraph) |>
  arrange(countries_concerned, body, document_publication_date, title_2, paragraph) |>
  rename(
    state_under_review     = countries_concerned,
    document_code          = document,
    session                = upr_session,
    cycle                  = upr_cycle,
    recommending_state_upr = upr_reccomending_states,
    response_upr           = upr_position
  ) |>
  # Fix two states that imported with a trailing partner name.
  mutate(state_under_review = case_when(
    state_under_review == "South Sudan - Sudan"   ~ "South Sudan",
    state_under_review == "Uzbekistan - Czechia"  ~ "Czechia",
    .default = state_under_review
  )) |>
  # Enrich with ISO codes.
  left_join(
    state_geo |> select(country, iso3, iso2) |> st_drop_geometry(),
    by = join_by(state_under_review == country)
  )
```

## 4. Build the UPR subset

```{r}
#| label: build-upr
upr_df <- df |>
  filter(body == "UPR") |>
  droplevels() |>
  mutate(
    recommending_state_upr    = str_remove(recommending_state_upr, "^- "),
    recommending_states_count = if_else(
      is.na(recommending_state_upr), NA_real_,
      str_count(recommending_state_upr, "-") + 1
    ),
    text_2 = strip_prefix(text)
  ) |>
  # Drop non-recommendation rows.
  filter(
    !str_starts(text, "Response of "),
    response_upr != "Concerns/Observations"
  ) |>
  rowid_to_column() |>
  # Comma- and semicolon-separated renderings of the recommending-state list.
  mutate(
    recommending_state_upr_comma     = collapse_states(recommending_state_upr, ", "),
    recommending_state_upr_semicolon = collapse_states(recommending_state_upr, "; ")
  ) |>
  mutate(recommending_state_upr_semicolon = recommending_state_upr_semicolon |>
    str_replace("State of Palestine\\*", "Palestine") |>
    str_replace("Holy See", "Vatican") |>
    str_replace("Timor; Leste", "Timor-Leste")
  ) |>
  relocate(text1, text_2, .after = text) |>
  relocate(
    recommending_state_upr_comma, recommending_state_upr_semicolon, recommending_states_count,
    .after = recommending_state_upr
  ) |>
  mutate(response_upr = factor(
    case_when(
      provisional ~ "Response not available",
      response_upr == "Supported/Noted" ~ "Partially supported",
      .default = response_upr
    ),
    levels = c("Supported", "Partially supported", "Noted", "Response not available")
  ))
```

For the present analysis, the dataset was downloaded on
`r max(upr_df$date_accessed, na.rm = TRUE)`. The UPR subset holds
`r format(nrow(upr_df), big.mark = ",")` recommendations.

## 5. Save

```{r}
#| label: save
# saveRDS(df,     here("data", "UHRI_full.rds"))
# saveRDS(df,     here("data", paste0("UHRI_full_", Sys.Date(), ".rds")))
saveRDS(upr_df, here("data", "UHRI_UPR.rds"))
saveRDS(upr_df, here("data", paste0("UHRI_UPR_", Sys.Date(), ".rds")))
```
